biggest_blocker: Every visitor sees the same static hero
desired_outcome: One route that adapts to stage and role
hero_headline: Maya, make onboarding pages adapt as fast as your roadmap.
sub_headline: OrbitStack can keep one page shell while each visitor sees copy tuned to product stage, role, and intent.
proof: Series B product teams use deterministic property zones to get relevant copy on screen before any live generation.
value_prop: Your website can finally reflect CRM context, feature adoption, and rollout timing without multiplying page variants.
notes: Prefers low-friction rollout paths and measurable experiments.

primary_goal: Validate the edge stack before rollout
biggest_blocker: Personalization logic is opaque and hard to audit
desired_outcome: Deterministic rollout path with visible traces
hero_headline: Daniel, validate the edge path before you scale the rollout.
sub_headline: Northstar can test deterministic property zones, grouped outputs, and event capture on one route without replacing the site architecture.
proof: Architecture teams favor the slug-based path because it is deterministic, auditable, and easy to QA against specific records.
value_prop: The flagship page surfaces raw stream timing, identify calls, memorize writes, and event beacons so operations stay inspectable.
notes: Needs observable, low-risk validation before production traffic.

biggest_blocker: Too many page variants to maintain
desired_outcome: One canonical page with sharper campaign copy
hero_headline: Aisha, stop cloning pages just to sharpen campaign fit.
sub_headline: VectorLoop can let one route speak differently to lifecycle, segment, and source without breaking the visual system.
proof: Growth teams use flat zones to tailor proof and objections per campaign while keeping the hero deterministic.
value_prop: Blend stored audience context with generated proof so paid traffic, outbound traffic, and product-led return visits stop feeling identical.
notes: Optimizes campaigns and wants fewer page variants.

primary_goal: Ship one adaptive page without a framework rewrite
biggest_blocker: Personalization feels too heavy for a small stack
desired_outcome: Static HTML that still feels adaptive
hero_headline: Leo, make your site smarter without turning it into a framework project.
sub_headline: Sidebuild can keep static HTML, add text-only zones, and still connect memory, prompts, and a visible event loop.
proof: Developer-tool founders like the zero-dependency example because it reads as plain HTML first and product logic second.
value_prop: You can start with one file, one script, and a debug panel that proves the backend is working before you automate anything else.
notes: Keeps scope narrow and values explicit contracts.
